title: Is this good NGE merch that I want
display_title: Is this good NGE merch that I want
wikitext:
[]: Is this good NGE merch that I want?             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Tue Jul 18, 2006 4:37 pm So I watch eps 1-4 on youtube. I go to wikipedia, learn more about NGE, like the angels, LCL, yada yada yada. I start to really like NGE. So here's what I plan to get. -Platinum Collection -Wallscrolls -Rei in a kimono, holding a fan, moon in bg. -Rei holding a red, twisted, dual-pronged spear and plugsuit-clad. -A poster of Rei in a striped bathing suit. - Franchise fans are not w/o their figures, FYI. So how's that? If there's any nice, (but $<100) merchandise related to NGE that no Evangelion fan can be without, post some suggestions.

[]:             Quote Post by System Shocker » Tue Jul 18, 2006 5:12 pm Hey now, Platinum Collection is no longer the latest edition of Evangelion. You should pre-order the limited 10th Anniversary of Neon Genesis Evangelion, since Platinum Collection had no bonus features and this comes out a month from now. It comes with at least one humper sticker and a jacket. The form for the jacket comes in the box, and you haveto send it back in the mail to tell them what size you want to wear it. We don't know yet how many more bonus and commemorative items will be coming with it, but I have my hopes up. Normal price is 250-300 dollars, but dvdpacific.com has it for 144 [url]http://www.dvdpacific.com/item.asp?ID=735541[/url] If you decide to buy the Platinum now, you might regret it later that you didn't get the newer thing a month before. Another thing I have is a figure of one of the Evas from the series for 15 dollars [url]http://www.ncsxshop.com/cgi-bin/shop/EVA-02MV.html[/url] and you might even be interested in buying this silverish thing [url]http://www.entertainmentearth.com/prodinfo.asp?number=DC80579[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Tue Jul 18, 2006 7:34 pm Hey hey hey, you found it on you tube? I had no such luck. Point me in the right direction.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 18, 2006 7:46 pm ZeroFive1 wrote: Hey hey hey, you found it on you tube? I had no such luck. Point me in the right direction.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Tue Jul 18, 2006 7:51 pm System Shocker wrote: Hey now, Platinum Collection is no longer the latest edition of Evangelion. You should pre-order the limited 10th Anniversary of Neon Genesis Evangelion, since Platinum Collection had no bonus features and this comes out a month from now. It comes with at least one humper sticker and a jacket. The form for the jacket comes in the box, and you haveto send it back in the mail to tell them what size you want to wear it. We don't know yet how many more bonus and commemorative items will be coming with it, but I have my hopes up. Normal price is 250-300 dollars, but dvdpacific.com has it for 144 [url]http://www.dvdpacific.com/item.asp?ID=735541[/url] http://www.evamonkey.com/uploaded_images/tenth_anniversary_box-778622.jpg If you decide to buy the Platinum now, you might regret it later that you didn't get the newer thing a month before.

[]:             Quote Post by System Shocker » Tue Jul 18, 2006 10:36 pm Under no circumstances will you buy the movies from stores in America. The company who licensed it in America sells both the dvds in one package and the video quality is of a VHS master. The renewal dvds are as far as I know the only remastered version worth having on dvd and it is only available in Japan. You could get the ZX Evangelion torrent which has the entire Renewal series and movies and the fandub parody. They used the Renewal dvds and resynched the video to match up that of the american Platinum Edition English audio. Everything except for Redeath fandub parody are at h.264 video quality or higher. And that's really good. You might try and find the ZX Evangelion thing before 10th Anniversary Edition comes out. Just type 'zx evangelion filetype:torrent' into normal google search.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Tue Jul 18, 2006 11:45 pm i've got the ZX and it seems same w/ my platinum :? If you decide to buy the Platinum now, you might regret it later that you didn't get the newer thing a month before. Another thing I have is a figure of one of the Evas from the series for 15 dollars

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Jul 18, 2006 11:51 pm I don't own any Eva merch except for my Platinum Thinpak and my D&R/EoE 2-Pack. :( I'd like to get one of those awesome model kits, but they're crazy expensive. My brother recieved the one of Unit 01 as a birthday gift a few years ago, and it was crazy cool, even though IMO he could have put a little more effort into it. I don't think I'm obsessed enough to have little plastic figures of Asuka and Rei in bikinis cluttering my home's flat surfaces though. :p

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 19, 2006 12:34 am System Shocker wrote: Under no circumstances will you buy the dvds from stores in America. The company who licensed it in America sells both the dvds in one package and the video quality is of a VHS master.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jul 19, 2006 5:42 am OK, so the scoop is this; There was a Renewal in 2003, and they remastered the series, Death, and EoE: Air/My Purest Heart For You. ADV got the remastered series and released them as the Platinum version, and kept the original TV-versions intact too (the "Director's Cuts" was really the only ones released on video in Japan). Death and EoE are licensed by Manga Entertainment, who are famous for getting licenses just for the sake of not allowing anyone else to release it. They're selling an old video transfer, making money of it, and would have to pay big bucks for the Renewal. So the english Renewal movies are not gonna happen unless A) The license runs out, or B) A miracle happens.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Wed Jul 19, 2006 6:41 am I don't mind not having remastered movies at all. It's fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jul 19, 2006 6:52 am I do, when the quality difference is this great

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 19, 2006 10:44 am Kaysow wrote: ADV got the remastered series and released them as the Platinum version, and kept the original TV-versions intact too

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Wed Jul 19, 2006 10:59 am Whatever, man. In other areas, what should I get in terms of figures? Should I get the set I posted previously or get a different one? I want one of one of the three pilots, an EVA, and an Angel. I want it to be pre-made and not one of those sets that you waste painstaking amounts of time building a model.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jul 19, 2006 1:25 pm I never did see the series before the Platinum version. I just see "normal" and "DC" on it, and people tell me it's an added bonus because ADV were so eager to get things out in the US that they released OA versions of the final episodes, before the Japanese LD/VHS version were produced. An admirable thing to do for the fans, if I might add. I guess those were the pre-Platinum ones then :?

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Wed Jul 19, 2006 7:02 pm Enough with the DVDs and jazz. Could someone reccomend me some good figures?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jul 19, 2006 7:12 pm Take a look at eBay, they've got TONS.

[]:             Quote Post by System Shocker » Wed Jul 19, 2006 10:44 pm [url]http://froogle.google.com/froogle?btnG=Search+Froogle&lmode=online&q=evangelion+action+figure&lnk=qsugt[/url] Froogle Google is better

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Wed Jul 19, 2006 10:48 pm As far as figures go, I always like to take time and pimp the kubrick figures. The angels, evas, and characters in convienent chibi lego format.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 20, 2006 2:18 am Kaysow wrote: I never did see the series before the Platinum version. I just see "normal" and "DC" on it , and people tell me it' s an added bonus because ADV were so eager to get things out in the US that they released OA versions of the final episodes, before the Japanese LD/VHS version were produced.
